user_message: Is squatting good for building glutes?

stream_iterations.text:
Yes, squats are solid for glute development, but with a couple of caveats worth knowing.

Squats do hit the glutes, especially in the bottom of the movement where the glutes are stretched and under load — that deep range is where a lot of the growth stimulus comes from. Going to at least parallel, and ideally a bit below, makes a real difference here. High-bar and low-bar back squats both work, though some lifters find a slightly wider stance with toes turned out gives them a better glute feel.

That said, squats are fundamentally a quad-dominant movement, so if your primary goal is glute hypertrophy, they work best as part of a mix rather than your only tool. Exercises like Romanian deadlifts, hip thrusts, and Bulgarian split squats tend to load the glutes through a longer range of motion and can be more directly targeted. The research generally supports using both compound movements like squats and more glute-specific work together for maximum hypertrophy.

So short answer: yes, keep squatting, but pair them with some dedicated glute exercises and you'll get a lot more out of your training.
